about.title: About
about.intro: Cognipedia is a playful encyclopedia of cognitive biases. Discover how your brain tricks you through interactive situations and quizzes.
about.codex: Cognitive biases are organized into 4 families, inspired by the Cognitive Bias Codex:
about.codex.too-much-information: Too much information — our brain filters and only retains part of the available information.
about.codex.not-enough-meaning: Not enough meaning — we fill in the gaps by inventing stories and patterns.
about.codex.need-to-act-fast: Need to act fast — to avoid missing opportunities, we take shortcuts.
about.codex.what-to-remember: What to remember? — our memory reinforces some memories and alters others.
error.404.messages: Blind spot bias — you were convinced this page existed.
Dunning-Kruger effect — this URL was confident, but wrong.
Confirmation bias — you wanted to believe, but this page doesn't exist.
Frequency illusion — no, you haven't seen this page before.
Halo effect — that link looked trustworthy, yet it leads nowhere.
